name: Tretinoin
chemistry: all-trans retinoic acid · C<sub>20</sub>H<sub>28</sub>O<sub>2</sub> · MW 300.4 Da
lead: Prescription all-trans retinoic acid. The most-studied topical retinoid in dermatology — and the standard against which every other anti-aging molecule is measured.

whatItIs.body: eady-to-bind retinoic acid (MW ~300 Da) — it skips the two oxidation steps that retinol must complete inside skin cells. It preferentially binds RAR-γ and RAR-β nuclear receptors in fibroblasts and keratinocytes, shifting transcription toward new collagen synthesis, faster corneocyte turnover, and melanocyte suppression.
whatItIs.body2: It is the most comprehensively documented topical for photoageing reversal — the molecule that established the entire category. When studies say "retinoid," they almost always mean tretinoin or its derivatives.

mechanism.b: Tretinoin → RAR-γ / RAR-β in keratinocytes & fibroblasts.
Up-regulates pro-collagen I & III, down-regulates MMP-1.
Thickened dermis, faster turnover, tone evened over months.

studies.finding: 0.05% tretinoin cream improved photoaging signs (fine wrinkling, mottled hyperpigmentation, sallowness, tactile roughness) significantly over vehicle in a double-blind randomised controlled trial.
0.02% tretinoin significantly improved fine wrinkles, mottling, roughness, and laxity over 24 weeks at the lowest commercially available concentration.
Topical retinoids remain first-line for comedonal and mild inflammatory acne, with tretinoin and adapalene having the strongest evidence base.
Tretinoin remains the gold-standard topical retinoid for photoaging reversal, with histologic and clinical evidence accumulated over four decades.

byConcern.note: 0.025% is the sweet spot for tolerability and results — most patients should never need higher.
Pair with BP short-contact to reduce resistance risk.
Often combined with oral therapy for moderate-severe acne.
Slow but durable; combine with sunscreen and azelaic acid.
Only the red phase responds. Older silver striae do not.

howToUse.body: Begin every third night for two weeks, then every other night for two weeks, then nightly. If irritation: drop one rung.
Pea-sized for the entire face. Less is not safer; less is just under-dosed and slower to results.
Apply to dry skin (10 min after washing). Buffer with moisturiser sandwich if sensitive — moisturiser → tretinoin → moisturiser.
SPF 30+ broad-spectrum every morning. This is non-negotiable on tretinoin.

mistakes.b: Damp skin amplifies penetration and irritation. Wait 10 minutes after washing.

mistakes.t: Layering with strong AHAs the same night
mistakes.b: Acid-on-retinoid stacks the irritation. Alternate nights.
Start at 0.025% every third night. Escalate over weeks.

mistakes.t: Stopping after a week of peeling
mistakes.b: Peeling is the expected acclimation phase, not failure.

mistakes.t: Using a tiny dot for the whole face
mistakes.b: Pea-sized. Less is not safer; less is just under-dosed.

myths.m: Tretinoin is too harsh for dry skin.
myths.t: Dry skin needs more buffering — moisturiser sandwich, alternate nights — but benefits equally from long-term use.

myths.m: Natural retinol alternatives work as well.
myths.t: Bakuchiol shows promising retinoid-like gene modulation but does not match tretinoin's RCT evidence base.

myths.m: You can't use tretinoin in summer.
myths.t: You can. Don't stop. Just be meticulous with broad-spectrum SPF — that's true year-round anyway.
It thins the stratum corneum (good — that's how it works) and thickens the dermis (also good — that's the anti-aging effect).

products.name: Tretinoin 0.025% Cream
products.note: Multiple Indian generics; Galderma & Sun Pharma most reliable.

faq.a: Acne improvements at 8–12 weeks. Photoaging improvements at 24–48 weeks of consistent use. Anything you notice in week one is mostly the acclimation phase.
Yes — long-term studies show maintained benefit without tolerance loss. Use the lowest effective frequency after the acclimation phase.
Older formulations were inactivated by BP. Modern tretinoin microsphere and lotion formulations are BP-compatible, or simply alternate nights.
Fine — do not stop. Just be meticulous with broad-spectrum SPF.
In most markets, yes. Some markets sell it OTC (e.g., parts of South-East Asia). A dermatologist consult is strongly preferred.
Classic tretinoin degrades in the presence of BP. Modern microsphere or polymeric emulsions of tretinoin are stable with BP, but alternating nights remains the safest approach.

reviewer.name: Dr. Sundeep
reviewer.title: Medical Review Lead
reviewer.quote: Tretinoin remains the highest-evidence anti-aging molecule we recommend. Pregnancy is the only absolute contraindication. Everything else is a tolerability question — and tolerability is solved by dose and frequency, not by abandoning the molecule.
